title: Implement Bootstrap Wizard and lifecycle schema
rawUserRequest: 继续推进当前文档工单；所有计划工单按完整流程执行，不跳过工单，并完成 PR、合并、ai-close-work-item 与分支清理。

declaredIntent.summary: Make Bootstrap Adoption a distinct, resumable lifecycle with explicit schema fields and a fail-closed readiness boundary.

outOfScope: Ownership Manifest and full update/rollback/disable/enable/uninstall implementation (WI8)
Starting WI8 or any later plan Work Item

sources.reason: WI7 acceptance requires Detect → Propose → Y/N → Select/Input → Review → Drift Revalidation → Confirm → Write, explicit bootstrap schema, and no Ready claim.
Existing side-effect-free wizard is the bounded transition seam for lifecycle behavior.

riskAssessment.reason: Bootstrap must remain resumable and write-capable only after explicit human confirmation, without being mistaken for governed development readiness.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized the complete serial plan implementation and its required governance lifecycle.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Wizard supports Detect, Propose, Configure/Select-Input, Review, Confirm, Write, Back, Cancel, Resume, and drift revalidation with explicit transitions.
Bootstrap records validate lifecycleType=bootstrap_adoption and creationMode=bootstrap_installer, and cannot satisfy governed-development readiness.
Bootstrap writes remain fail-closed until explicit human confirmation and preserve adoption evidence without creating a standard development Work Item.
Regression tests, capability truth, and installation documentation describe the lifecycle boundary accurately.
guidelines: Bootstrap state and schema changes must preserve the not-ready boundary.
Write transitions require explicit confirmation and drift validation.
Keep WI8 ownership/lifecycle implementation out of this Work Item.

intent.problem: The repository has wizard and write-boundary primitives, but Bootstrap Adoption is not yet a complete explicit lifecycle with a schema that cannot be mistaken for governed development.
constraints: Do not implement WI8 full ownership/update/rollback/uninstall behavior.
Do not claim Bootstrap or Calibration is Ready.
Do not start any later plan Work Item.
intent.rationale: A strict lifecycle schema and transition contract makes confirmation, resume, drift, and readiness boundaries auditable before later installed-lifecycle work.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Bootstrap session completes only after explicit confirmation and write boundary
Bootstrap cancellation, resume, and drift invalidate downstream decisions
Bootstrap lifecycle facts cannot claim governed readiness

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the complete plan implementation, including governance files, PRs, merges, cleanup, and release.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
